# Graceful Degradation

## The Physics

Systems fail. The question is not WHETHER failure occurs but HOW the system
behaves when it does. Gracefully degrading systems lose capability incrementally,
maintaining core function while shedding non-essential features. Brittle systems
fail completely when any component fails.

Applied to governance: if a review cadence is missed, the object should be flagged
as stale — not silently trusted and not catastrophically deleted.

## Why It Matters

- **Resilience**: Partial service is better than no service during failures.
- **Recovery**: Graceful degradation preserves the state needed for recovery.
- **Trust**: Users trust systems that bend rather than break.
- **Diagnosis**: Partial failure reveals which component failed. Total failure hides everything.

## Academic/Industry Backing

- Resilience engineering (Hollnagel): designing for graceful degradation under stress.
- Circuit breaker pattern (Nygard, "Release It!"): isolate failing components.
- Aviation safety: redundant systems ensure partial capability on component failure.

## Beyond Survival: Antifragility

Graceful degradation is the floor — survive failure without collapse. Antifragility
is the ceiling — grow STRONGER from stress. Nassim Taleb's insight: some systems
don't just resist shocks, they need them. Muscles grow from strain. Immune systems
strengthen from exposure. Governance systems that conduct post-incident reviews and
update their standards are antifragile — every failure makes the system harder to
break next time.

The progression: Fragile (breaks under stress) → Robust (resists stress) →
Resilient (recovers from stress) → Antifragile (improves from stress). JOS aims
for antifragility: every governance failure, every missed review, every stale
object is a signal that strengthens the system when acted upon. The health model,
compliance-outcome divergence detection, and review cadence enforcement are all
mechanisms that convert stress into improvement.

Antifragility requires that failure is visible (JOS-P15), that learning happens
(JOS-P08), and that evolution follows evidence (JOS-P23). Without these, failure
is just damage.

## Connected Objects

- JOS-P04 (Mandatory Escalation) — escalation is a graceful degradation mechanism
- JOS-C10 (Emergent Complexity) — emergent systems degrade more gracefully than prescribed ones
- JOS-C28 (Autopoiesis) — self-maintaining systems are a prerequisite for antifragility
- JOS-P08 (Audit and Learn) — the learning loop that converts stress into strength
- JOS-P23 (Evidence-Based Evolution) — evidence-driven change is how antifragility manifests

### Framework Connection

Framework §9 (lifecycle states as an orthogonal axis) provides the state model through which degradation is expressed, while §10 (compliance-outcome divergence detection) gives the system the ability to detect when degradation is occurring and route corrective action.
